description: Introductory course covering neural networks (CNN, RNN, Auto-encoder), Reinforcement Learning, and Transformers. Students learn to use TensorFlow and Keras libraries while designing custom networks and developing data science analysis skills.
R-based analysis of healthcare data spanning EHR, claims, and registry data. Bridges programming knowledge with healthcare analytics.
First-semester capstone design course for Biomedical Software Engineering majors. Students plan and prototype a healthcare-focused software project.
Graduate seminar on the design of AI-powered digital health systems. Covers system architecture, model integration, and clinical deployment considerations.

Bridges programming knowledge with healthcare analytics. Covers medical data ecosystems (EHR, insurance claims, registry data), statistical methods (regression, survival analysis, hypothesis testing), medical coding systems (ICD, SNOMED-CT), ethical research considerations, and R programming proficiency for medical data analysis.

Comprehensive introduction to statistical learning with R applications. Covers supervised learning (regression, classification, tree-based methods) and unsupervised learning. Introductory statistics recommended.

Python-based course designed for students from healthcare backgrounds with limited programming experience. Covers Python basics, data analysis tools, machine learning concepts, clinical data processing, and medical imaging applications.

Introductory course covering neural networks (CNN, RNN, Auto-encoder), Reinforcement Learning, and Transformers. Students learn to use TensorFlow and Keras libraries while designing custom networks and developing data science analysis skills.
Beginner Python course introducing computing concepts and programming fundamentals. Covers basic data types, functional and object-oriented programming techniques.
